channel.title: nlin.PS updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: nlin.PS updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Amplitude-Defect Mediated Transition to Partial Incoherence -- From Experiment to Theory
item.description: arXiv:2607.15924v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phase-only models have contributed significantly to the understanding of synchronization; however, they do not account for dynamical scenarios where amplitude dynamics matter. This study identifies an amplitude-mediated transition from complete frequency coherence to partial incoherence, observed both in an electrochemical silicon-etching experiment and within a population of globally coupled heterogeneous Stuart-Landau oscillators. Strong coupling introduces a bimodal-amplitude distribution from which the transition to partial incoherence is triggered by successive secondary Hopf bifurcations in low-amplitude oscillators. When these modulations cause oscillators to experience amplitude defects, the winding number changes, converting the secondary frequency into a new, oscillator-specific mean frequency. This mechanism results in a partially incoherent state, in which one amplitude group maintains frequency locking while another develops a dispersed frequency branch. These findings demonstrate that amplitude defects offer a pathway to incoherence that phase-only models cannot capture.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Thom\'e, Yukiteru Murakami, Yannick Sch\"ohs, Katharina Krischer

item.title: Constructing far-from-equilibrium patterns in a cross-diffusion vegetation-autotoxicity model
item.description: arXiv:2607.15692v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Using geometric singular perturbation theory, we construct stationary, periodic, front-type far-from-equilibrium patterns in a cross-diffusion model for plant growth under the influence of toxicity. We show how existing techniques for the analysis of far-from-equilibrium patterns in one spatial dimension can be extended to include cross-diffusion terms and prove the existence of a one-parameter family of these patterns. For a general cross-diffusion model class, we show when front-type patterns may appear depending on the shape of the critical manifold, which is determined by the specific properties of the reaction terms.
item.dc:creator: Annalisa Iuorio, Cinzia Soresina, Frits Veerman
